sections.paragraph:
ἐξηχέω (s. ἠχέω) fut. 3 sg. ἐξηχήσει Sir 40:13; aor. 3 pl. ἐξήχησαν Jer 3:14; pf. pass. ἐξήχημαι (mostly act. intr. ‘sound forth’: Jo 3:14; Sir 40:13; Philo, In Flacc. 39, Rer. Div. Her. 15, Dec. 46) trans., ‘cause to resound or be heard’ (Polyb. 30, 4, 7; Philo, Abr. 180) pass. be caused to sound forth, ring out (3 Macc 3:2) ἀφʼ ὑμῶν ἐξήχηται ὁ λόγος τοῦ κυρίου the word of the Lord has sounded forth fr. you 1 Th 1:8.—DELG s.v. ἠχή.

fut. fut. = fut.

  1. sg. = singular

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. pl. = plural
  2. pf. = perfect

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

act. act. = active

intr. intr. = intransitive

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

trans. trans. = transitive

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

  1. fr. = from

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 349
